name: Beef and Broccoli
recipeIngredient: 1/2 lb Boneless beef (Flank steak, top sirloin, or tenderloin), sliced across grain into thin slices
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine all marinade ingredients (2 tbsp oyster sauce, 2 tsp rice wine, 2 tsp brown sugar, and 2 tsp cornstarch dissolved in water) in a bowl.
2. Mix beef with the marinade and marinate for 30 minutes.
3. Heat a wok over high heat. When hot, swirl in 3 tbsp peanut oil.
4. Add ginger and salt, then toss for a few seconds.
5. Add broccoli and stir-fry for one minute.
6. Add water and turn the heat to medium-high. Cover and steam until the broccoli is crisp-tender.
7. Uncover and turn the heat to high. Stir-fry until the water is evaporated.
8. Remove the broccoli and reserve it.
9. Reheat the same wok (without rinsing) and, when hot, add 2 more tablespoons of peanut oil.
10. Add beef slices and stir-fry until lightly browned but not cooked through. Avoid stirring too vigorously; press slices against the sides of the wok to sear.
11. Add broccoli back to the wok and toss.
12. When ready, stir in the seasoning sauce (2 tsp rice wine, 2 tsp brown sugar, and 2 tsp cornstarch dissolved in water). Stir until the sauce thickens.
13. Turn onto a platter and drizzle with sesame oil. The sugar in the marinade and seasoning sauce enhances the flavor without adding sweetness.
